theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: The Volumetric Principle: Stillness, Buoyancy, and the Elsasser Attractor Primary Registry IDs: 024 (TZP vacuum energy density — the vacuum is neutrally buoyant in the TZP geometry), 853 (Lyapunov stability — the Elsasser attractor is the Lyapunov stable equilibrium of MHD systems) Secondary IDs: 001–004 (manifold structure — geodesic motion is volumetric equilibration in the Trawinistic manifold) Tertiary IDs: 012–013 (field coherence and null space projection — the volumetric principle selects the null space configuration as the equilibrium) is the mathematical statement that $\Phi \text{target}$ is a stable attractor — all perturbations decay toward it.
theory.technical_interpretation: ng force). The Lyapunov stability of $\Lambda = 1$ follows from the stability of neutral buoyancy. --- ## Geodesic Motion and the Trawinistic Manifold (IDs 001–003) The chapter's identification of geodesic motion as volumetric equilibration connects to the manifold structure entries directly. ID 001 defines the Trawinistic manifold as a smooth manifold $(M, g)$ with Lorentzian metric. The Lorentzian signature $(-,+,+,+)$ means that the metric is not positive-definite — there is a preferred time direction. Geodesic motion (free fall) is motion that extremises the proper time functional: **The extremisation of proper time is the gravitational analogue of volumetric equilibration**: just as neutral buoyancy minimises the displacement of fluid by maximising the match between body density and fluid density, geodesic motion minimises the displacement of the body in spacetime by maximising the In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

dictionary_sections.definition: The Volumetric Principle is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation \tau = \int\sqrt{-g_{\mu\nu}\,dx^\mu\,dx^\nu}. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: Chapter: The Volumetric Principle: Stillness, Buoyancy, and the Elsasser Attractor Primary Registry IDs: 024 (TZP vacuum energy density — the vacuum is neutrally buoyant in the TZP geometry), 853 (Lyapunov stability — the Elsasser attractor is the Lyapunov stable equilibrium of MHD systems) Secondary IDs: 001–004 (manifold structure — geodesic motion is volumetric equilibration in the Trawinistic manifold) Tertiary IDs: 012–013 (field coherence and null space projection — the volumetric principle selects the null space configuration as the equilibrium) is the mathematical statement that $\Phi \text{target}$ is a stable attractor — all perturbations decay toward it. The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records \tau = \int\sqrt{-g_{\mu\nu}\,dx^\mu\,dx^\nu}; V[\Phi] = \|\Phi - \Phi_\text{target}\|^2, \quad \frac{dV}{dt} < 0; \hat{H}|\Psi_\text{THZ}\rangle = E_\text{Zenith}|\Psi_\text{THZ}\rangle; \hat{H}_\text{optical}|\Psi\rangle = E_p|\Psi\rangle, \quad \hat{H}_\text{charge}|\Psi\rangle = E_e|\Psi\rangle, \quad \hat{H}_\text{vortex}|\Psi\rangle = E_v|\Psi\rangle. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The supporting lemma is source locality: the selected relation appears in Volumetric_Principle_COMPANION_REGISTRY_NOTE.md, and the adjacent context connects it to the title, domain, and surrounding argument. The packet therefore treats the formula, its explanatory paragraph, and its neighboring equations as one evidence cluster.

dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: The Volumetric Principle extends the registry beyond the first thousand IDs by giving the new band a cited mathematical object, a text-rich dictionary page, and a derivation path attached to the exact source file.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
\tau = \int\sqrt{-g_{\mu\nu}\,dx^\mu\,dx^\nu}
\]
This line is the 1 source equation retained from Volumetric\_Principle\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Volumetric Principle packet.
\item
\[
V[\Phi] = \|\Phi - \Phi_\text{target}\|^2, \quad \frac{dV}{dt} < 0
\]
This line is the 2 source equation retained from Volumetric\_Principle\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Volumetric Principle packet.
\item
\[
\hat{H}|\Psi_\text{THZ}\rangle = E_\text{Zenith}|\Psi_\text{THZ}\rangle
\]
This line is the 3 source equation retained from Volumetric\_Principle\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Volumetric Principle packet.
\item
\[
\hat{H}_\text{optical}|\Psi\rangle = E_p|\Psi\rangle, \quad \hat{H}_\text{charge}|\Psi\rangle = E_e|\Psi\rangle, \quad \hat{H}_\text{vortex}|\Psi\rangle = E_v|\Psi\rangle
\]
This line is the 4 source equation retained from Volumetric\_Principle\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Volumetric Principle packet.
\item
\[
V[\Lambda] = (\Lambda - 1)^2
\]
This line is the 5 source equation retained from Volumetric\_Principle\_COMPANION\_REGISTRY\_NOTE.md for the The Volumetric Principle packet.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
